review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-123-claude-review-output-valid.json

parsed_review.recommendation: Approve CP00-123 closeout. The pack satisfies every required permission/audit terminal boundary and no-write/no-leak constraint, with comprehensive self-validation in both the test suite and the contract validator.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope and coverage are correct: the catalog enumerates exactly the 10 planned units RP02.P06.M05.S21-RP02.P06.M06.S08 (2 in M05: cross-tenant + leak-prevention tests; 8 in M06: permission matrix row plus view/search/mutation/export/share/AI/audit-hint bindings), and the manifest/validator assert first=S21, last=S08, micro-phase counts 2/8, and deliverable counts test:2/security_audit:2/implementation:6. CP122 inheritance is real and verified (createPermissionKernelCp123PermissionAuditTerminalBoundary invokes createPermissionKernelCp122PermissionMatrixWorkflowBinding and runPermissionKernelCp122PermissionMatrixWorkflowCase; inherited_workflow_case_count===40 and inherited leak status is asserted). Security boundaries hold: cross-tenant routes through evaluatePermission with resource tenant t_other and fails closed with reason cross_tenant_deny and audit_hint.object_id 'redacted_cross_tenant_object' (asserted in both validate fn and contract script); leak/search trimming via trimSearchResults renders only ['d_cp123_document'], with unauthorized_count_exposed/hidden_field_names_exposed/hidden_fields_rendered all false and an explicit do_not_count_or_render_unauthorized_results policy, and privileged_note inputs are never mapped into output. Decision routing is correct and non-executing: mutation/share -> approval_required_routing with grants_human_approval=false; export/download -> review_required_routing with executes_export_download=false; AI -> blocked_ai_retrieval_boundary with executes_ai_retrieval=false. Preview-only and no-persist semantics are enforced: audit hints carry emitted_to_audit_ledger=false/preview_only=true, matrix_row.persisted=false, and stable replay sets replay_persists_state/replay_acquires_lock=false. No-write/no-leak constraints are met across the board: the new module is a pure src export (no runtime UI/API route added; runtime_ui_route_added/runtime_api_route_added=false), imports only pure evaluators from evaluate.js and the CP122 binding (no fs/network/db), and every result is sealed through freezeNoWriteResult with all 16 attestation flags false; the contract bumps to v0.17, updates current_pack to CP00-123/Risk A/10 units, and adds matching no_write_attestation, hidden_source_fields, and public_exports that the validator cross-checks. LDIP remains unimplemented and HRX stays embedded (hrx_embedded_boundary asserted), and handoff is fixed to CP00-124/RP02.P06.M06.S09 in the binding, contract, README, and validator. Behavioral correctness for cross-tenant redaction and ACL/tenant trimming legitimately delegates to the existing evaluate.js kernel (outside this diff), which is the intended reuse pattern and is pinned by the pack's own assertions; no reimplementation or boundary weakening was introduced.
